commit.message:
Decompiler: recover while(true) game loop via backward JMP detection (#258)

* Initial plan

* Decompiler: recover while(true) game loop via backward JMP detection

FindMainEnd() now detects backward JMP game loops (while(true){body})
in addition to JMP-to-self (while(true);). The last backward JMP
targeting an address within main, before the first RTS instruction,
is identified as the game loop end. DecompileMain() uses _mainEnd
to bound instruction collection and emits "while (true) ;" for
the terminal backward JMP.

commit.message:
Transpiler: support complex array index expressions (#234)

* Initial plan

* Support complex array index expressions in HandleLdelemU1

Add HandleLdelemU1ComplexIndex method that handles patterns like
array[(x >> 3) + ((y >> 3) << 4)] where the index is computed by
preceding arithmetic operations (shr, shl, add, sub, and, or, etc.).

The method walks backward through IL using stack depth tracking to
find the array instruction, removes array-load code from the block
buffer, and emits TAX + LDA array,X to access the element using the
computed index in the A register.

Add two new tests: ComplexArrayIndexExpression and SimpleShiftArrayIndex.

Co-authored-by: jonathanpeppers <840039+jonathanpeppers@users.noreply.github.com>

* Enhance test assertions for complex array index expressions

Add LSR, ASL, CLC assertions to verify the full expression is transpiled.

Co-authored-by: jonathanpeppers <840039+jonathanpeppers@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: preserve operand saves, strengthen test assertions

- Preserve STA TEMP and JSR pusha/pushax operand-saving instructions
  during block removal in HandleLdelemU1ComplexIndex, so prior runtime
  values survive across complex index computation
- Remove unconditional _savedRuntimeToTemp = false; the flag now
  correctly reflects whether a TEMP save exists in the block
- Replace single-byte hex assertions (4A, AA, BD) with multi-byte
  instruction sequences (4A4A4A, AABD, 0A0A0A0A) to eliminate
  false positives from operand bytes
- Add ComplexArrayIndex_PreservesOperandInTemp test verifying that
  (x - y) + arr[(byte)(x >> 3)] preserves the subtraction result

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix shoot2 score display: add per-frame VRAM updates (#233)

* Initial plan

* Fix shoot2 score display: add vrambuf VRAM updates each frame

Add vrambuf_clear()/set_vram_update(updbuf) during init, and
vrambuf_put()/vrambuf_clear() in the main loop so score digit
changes are pushed to the nametable every frame via NMI.

Co-authored-by: jonathanpeppers <840039+jonathanpeppers@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove pokes that clobber NAME_UPD_ENABLE via RAM mirror

poke(0x0804-0x0806) writes to NES RAM mirror of \-\
(NAME_UPD_ADR and NAME_UPD_ENABLE). The poke at \ sets
NAME_UPD_ENABLE to 0 every frame, preventing the NMI handler
from processing vrambuf_put data. Remove all three pokes since
vrambuf_clear() properly manages the update buffer.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: optimize extract_statement_type regex and encoding in helpers-mysql (#2088)

* perf: optimize extract_statement_type regex and encoding

Remove trailing .* from QUERY_NAME_REGEX to avoid scanning the entire
SQL string after matching the keyword. Only the first capture group
(the SQL operation) is used — the .* forced O(n) cost for no benefit.

Skip utf8_encode when the string is already valid UTF-8, which is the
common case for strings from ActiveRecord/Trilogy. Fall back to
binary: true encoding only for non-UTF-8 or invalid byte sequences.

Benchmarked improvements (combined):
- Short queries (53c):  1.91µs → 833ns  (2.3×)
- Long JOINs (768c):    6.97µs → 1.13µs (6.2×)
- IN clauses (2,406c): 15.19µs → 886ns  (17.1×)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Use modifier unless for single-line body
- Use unary plus (+) instead of .dup for unfreezing strings
- Use single-quoted strings where interpolation is not needed

  "--expect mock" gate before E2E tests; add multi-arch preflight

Add foundational infrastructure (VNet, Storage, Log Analytics, Key Vault) in Bicep and Terraform (#5)

* Initial plan

* feat(bicep): add foundational infrastructure modules

- modules/networking/virtualNetwork.bicep  — AVM vnet:0.7.2, PE subnet with privateEndpointNetworkPolicies=Disabled
- modules/storage/storageAccount.bicep     — AVM storage-account:0.9.1, allowSharedKeyAccess=false, publicNetworkAccess=Disabled, allowBlobPublicAccess=false
- modules/monitoring/logAnalyticsWorkspace.bicep — AVM operational-insights/workspace:0.9.1
- modules/security/keyVault.bicep          — AVM key-vault/vault:0.9.0, RBAC auth, soft-delete+purge protection, publicNetworkAccess=Disabled
- main.bicep                               — entry-point orchestrating all four modules
- parameters/main.dev.bicepparam           — dev environment parameter file

All files pass `bicep lint` with zero errors and zero warnings.

* feat(terraform): add foundational Azure infrastructure modules

Add Terraform modules for VNet, Storage Account, Log Analytics Workspace,
and Key Vault under src/terraform/, following AVM-first policy.

## Resources & AVM modules used
- Networking (VNet + PE subnet): Azure/avm-res-network-virtualnetwork/azurerm 0.17.1
- Storage Account (fully private): Azure/avm-res-storage-storageaccount/azurerm 0.6.7
- Log Analytics Workspace: Azure/avm-res-operationalinsights-workspace/azurerm 0.5.1
- Key Vault (RBAC + purge-protected): Azure/avm-res-keyvault-vault/azurerm 0.10.2

## Security hardening applied
- Storage: public_network_access_enabled=false, shared_access_key_enabled=false,
  allow_nested_items_to_be_public=false, TLS 1.2 minimum, HTTPS-only,
  network rules default-deny with no bypass
- Key Vault: RBAC authorization (legacy policies disabled), purge_protection=true,
  soft_delete_retention_days configurable, public_network_access=false,
  network_acls default-deny
- VNet subnet: private_endpoint_network_policies=Disabled,
  default_outbound_access_enabled=false

## Conventions
- CAF naming: rg-, vnet-, snet-pe-, st (alphanumeric), law-, kv-
- All resources tagged with common_tags (environment, workload, managed_by, repo, owner)
- Provider versions pinned with ~> (azurerm ~> 4.0, azapi ~> 2.4)
- Terraform >= 1.9 required
- Backend config commented-out template (Azure Blob Storage)
- OIDC authentication (use_oidc=true) for both azurerm and azapi providers
- tenant_id auto-detected via data.azurerm_client_config.current

Passes: terraform fmt -check -recursive && terraform validate

* fix(terraform): replace em dashes with ASCII hyphens in module comments

* fix: remove default_outbound_access_enabled = false from PE subnet
